channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: To Take Us to the Heavens, James Turrell Went Underground
item.description: The American artist’s most ambitious “skyspace” installation, opening in Denmark on Friday, invites viewers to connect with the cosmos both inner and outer.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Abend

media:content.media:credit: Mathias Eis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: In the installation, said the architect Morten Schmidt, “you look directly up into the cosmos. But at the same time, it makes you reflect inward.”

item.title: Mideast Live Updates: Lebanon Attacks and Delay in Talks Test U.S.-Iran Deal
item.description: Israel and Hezbollah traded new strikes in southern Lebanon, as Switzerland said it had postponed a round of U.S.-Iran talks previously set for Friday.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Near Nabatieh, Lebanon, on Friday after an airstrike.

item.title: Nos gustaba trabajar desde casa. Luego vimos los datos
item.description: A pesar de sus ventajas, el trabajo remoto ha agravado considerablemente el aislamiento y la angustia de los estadounidenses.
item.dc:creator: Emma Harrington and Natalia Emanuel

media:content.media:credit: Ilustración de The New York Times

item.title: U.K. Live Updates: Labour Mayor Wins Special Election, Clearing Path to Challenge Starmer
item.description: Andy Burnham, the Labour Party’s most popular politician, earned a decisive victory for a parliamentary seat in northern England. The win energizes his bid to become prime minister.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Temilade Adelaja/Reuters

item.title: Reseña de ‘Toy Story 5’: los dispositivos vienen por los niños
item.description: Jessie, Woody y Buzz Lightyear se enfrentan a una tableta electrónica que les roba la atención de la pequeña Bonnie.
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis

item.title: Irán gana concesiones económicas en el acuerdo inicial con EE. UU.
item.description: El país recibirá de nuevo millones de dólares conforme reabre el tráfico en el estrecho de Ormuz mientras que las negociaciones nucleares pasarán, por ahora, a un segundo plano.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Planet Labs Pbc, vía Reuters
media:content.media:description: Una imagen de satélite de la isla de Kharg, un centro petrolero iraní de vital importancia. El acuerdo concede a Irán exenciones para comenzar a exportar su petróleo incluso antes de que se negocie un acuerdo definitivo sobre su programa nuclear.

item.title: A Viral Knicks Moment, Brought to You by a Prediction Market
item.description: What do a Jalen Brunson look-alike contest, a buzzy catchphrase and a dancing robot outside Madison Square Garden have in common?
item.dc:creator: Troy Closson, Santul Nerkar and Lauren McCarthy

media:content.media:credit: Lexi Parra/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jake Epstein, a content creator, hosted a Jalen Brunson look-alike contest for Polymarket, a prediction market company, on Wednesday in Washington Square Park.

item.title: A Local Train System Is on a World Stage, and Everyone’s a Critic
item.description: NJ Transit has so far carried tens of thousands of fans to and from World Cup games in New Jersey without major problems, but many regular riders remain wary.
item.dc:creator: Stefanos Chen

media:content.media:credit: Adam Gray/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: NJ Transit sold fewer than 22,000 train tickets for the Brazil-Morocco match at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, N.J., last Saturday, about half the transit agency’s original per-game projection.

item.title: The (Very) Manhattan Moms Whose Sons Are Running for Congress
item.description: In a high-profile Democratic House primary, there’s a fair chance that would-be voters may have been sweet-talked by the proud mothers of leading candidates.
item.dc:creator: Matt Flegenheimer

media:content.media:credit: Heather Khalifa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lori Bores can be often seen wearing a sash identifying her as the mother of Alex Bores, a congressional candidate in Manhattan.

item.title: How the Pingry School in New Jersey Attracts World Cup Teams
item.description: The Pingry School hosted Italy at the 1994 World Cup. To prepare for Italy’s return this year, the school spent millions. And then Italy didn’t qualify.
item.dc:creator: David Waldstein

media:content.media:credit: Gabby Jones for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Morocco has made the Pingry School in New Jersey its home during the World Cup.

item.title: New York’s Dive Bars Have a Rich Past. Do They Have a Future?
item.description: Dive bars face an uncertain future with rising costs and changing social patterns.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kodé and Brian Karlsson

media:content.media:credit: Brian Karlsson for The New York Times

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 19, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: The Democracy of The Dive Bar
item.description: New York dive bars, known for their grit, have also been crucial spaces where people can mix across class over cheap beer, and sometimes organize and resist. Our reporter Anna Kodé describes how rising costs and a decline in drinking now threaten the survival of these establishments.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kodé, Gabriel Blanco, Haimy Assefa and Laura Salaberry

item.title: Swiss Officials Say Iran Talks Are Off, for Now
item.description: The announcement raised immediate questions over the fate of the agreement President Trump signed this week, with key details left to be worked out.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Pedestrians in Tehran on Monday. Talks on issues including Iran’s nuclear program have been postponed.

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Calls Trump’s Iran Deal ‘The Retreaty of Versailles’
item.description: “Trump’s answer to the question, ‘Who did you go to war with?’ and ‘How did it end?’ will be the same answer: ‘Iran,’” Kimmel said.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Reflecting Pool Turns Green, Paint Peels After Renovation
item.description: Algae blooms have hit the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, which underwent a $14.2 million repair project. Blue paint appeared to be chipping from the bottom.
item.dc:creator: Julie Yoon, Jackeline Luna and Alisa Shodiyev Kaff

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Peeled blue paint floating in the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool on Thursday.

item.title: Hannah Pingree and Bobby Charles Will Face Off in Maine Governor’s Race
item.description: Mr. Charles, a conservative Republican, had led the field ahead of a ranked-choice runoff. But Ms. Pingree, a former Maine House speaker, had been in a tight race with other Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell

item.title: Matt Dunlap Wins Democratic House Primary in Maine’s Swing District
item.description: Mr. Dunlap, a progressive Democrat, is set to face former Gov. Paul LePage, a Republican, in a competitive general election in November.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk and Bayliss Wagner

media:content.media:credit: Graeme Sloan/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Matt Dunlap, the state auditor, was the Maine secretary of state from 2013 to 2021.

item.title: Los europeos tienen algunas opiniones sobre la sauna en EE. UU.
item.description: Franceses, finlandeses y noruegos miran con cierto horror la forma en que los estadounidenses adaptan su milenaria práctica comunitaria.
item.dc:creator: Christer Wahlberg

item.title: Labour’s Andy Burnham Wins U.K. Special Election
item.description: Andy Burnham of the Labour Party won a key seat in the United Kingdom’s Parliament, clearing the path for him to challenge Keir Starmer as prime minister.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Oli Scarff/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: Art Galleries Are Not OK
item.description: Many gallerists are questioning the fundamentals of their business. It’s as if the art world they know has fallen off its axis.
item.dc:creator: Marc Spiegler

media:content.media:credit: Dmitry Kostyukov for The New York Times

item.title: What Does It All Mean? Once a Year, French Students Try to Explain.
item.description: The high school philosophy exam is a rite of passage for French students. This year included questions about Friedrich Nietzsche’s 1878 book, “Human, All Too Human.”
item.dc:creator: Catherine Porter

media:content.media:credit: Juliette Pavy for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: High school students in Paris preparing for a philosophy exam, in June.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Ukraine Hits Moscow With One of Biggest Drone Attacks of War
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Friday, June 19, 2026

item.title: Have You Bought a Tesla Recently? We Want to Hear From You.
item.description: Tesla sales are rebounding in Europe even though many people have negative feelings about Elon Musk, the company’s chief executive. We want to know why.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing

media:content.media:credit: Nam Y. Huh/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Tesla’s car sales took a big hit in Europe last year, in part because many people were turned off by the political views of the company’s chief executive, Elon Musk.

item.title: I Have Fallen in Love With My Doctor
item.description: But we have a huge age gap.
item.dc:creator: W. Blake Martin

media:content.media:credit: Brian Rea

item.title: Neither Wanted Marriage. That Changed After Three Weeks Apart.
item.description: When Alison Greenberg, a rom-com writer, and Barry Loudis met, both were divorced with no desire to remarry. “Joke’s on us,” Greenberg said.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Block

item.title: It Was Smooth Sailing From the Start
item.description: When Matthew Casale, a lieutenant with the U.S. Navy, connected with Emily Sherron, he was instantly smitten.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Menza

item.title: On a Ride to Her Gate, She Found Her Husband
item.description: Dr. Aneesha Gala was waiting for an airport train when she ran into a classmate who was with her family. The classmate’s brother would become Gala’s husband.
item.dc:creator: Stella Raine Chu

item.title: The Wedding Was Part of a Show, and the Show Was Part of the Wedding
item.description: Pietro Alexander and Sara Maliki began a new chapter of their lives by getting married in the SoHo art gallery they opened moments later.
item.dc:creator: Valeriya Safronova

item.title: He Gave Her a ‘Nontransferable’ Coupon for a Ring
item.description: Robert O’Connell, an interior designer, proposed to Aiyana Jaffe, a jewelry designer, with a 1957 silver box by Maria Pergay. He left the ring up to her.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: An Opera Performance With a Real-Life Happy Ending
item.description: Gideon Dabi and Alyssa Martin met five years ago while performing in a production of Rossini’s “La Cenerentola.” They quickly became friends.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: San Francisco Film Patrons Are Found Dead on Side of Highway
item.description: Judith Sheldon, 84, daughter of an Oscar-winning director, and her husband, Wylie, 86, were traveling to Oregon on a hot day. Their deaths are under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Heather Knight and Madaleine Rubin

item.title: Vance Delays Trip to Switzerland for Direct Talks on Iran Deal
item.description: Mr. Vance said earlier at a press briefing that he did not know if he would travel to Switzerland on Friday for the talks, where he was initially expected for a signing ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The White House late Thursday said Vice President JD Vance was delaying a trip to Switzerland for negotiations with Iran.

item.title: Union Behind California Billionaire Tax Offers to Reduce the Rate
item.description: The health care workers’ union that made it to the ballot with its plan to impose a 5 percent tax on billionaires’ wealth told Gov. Gavin Newsom it would accept a 2 percent levy instead.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall
item.category: Service Employees International Union-United Healthcare Workers West

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gov. Gavin Newsom of California has opposed the billionaires’ tax, saying that it would hurt the state’s economy.

item.title: Who Is Andy Burnham, the Man Who Could Be Britain’s Next Prime Minister?
item.description: Charismatic, northern and exuding a relaxed optimism, Mr. Burnham is a contrast to Keir Starmer. His allies hope he could mend Labour’s relationship with voters.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle

media:content.media:credit: Jon Super/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Andy Burnham, Labour’s most popular politician, arriving on Friday for the results announcement for the Makerfield by-election, in which he beat the populist right-wing Reform U.K. party.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 19, 2026
item.description: Jack Hatchett makes his debut.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Get a running start before leaping into today’s puzzle.

item.title: Firm Tied to Trump Donor Got No-Bid Contract to Clean Reflecting Pool
item.description: A White House spokeswoman said the president was not involved in selecting Greenwater Services, the business owned by a trust led by John J. Cafaro.
item.dc:creator: David A. Fahrenthold

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Peeled blue paint floating in the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool on Thursday.

item.title: Salsa Macha Pork Noodles
item.description: These noodles are inspired by mazemen, a brothless ramen that is sometimes tossed with a saucy pork mixture. The dish comes together in less than half an hour, making it the perfect upgrade to an instant-ramen weeknight dinner.

media:content.media:credit: David Malosh for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Simon Andrews.
media:content.media:description: Octavio Peña’s salsa macha pork noodles.

item.title: Trump’s Intelligence Pick Renews Debate: Is the Director of National Intelligence Needed?
item.description: The director of national intelligence’s office was created in the aftermath of the Sept. 11 attacks. Now some lawmakers want to shrink it.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: Office of the Director of National Intelligence

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Tulsi Gabbard, seen at a hearing in March, is stepping down as the director of the office of national intelligence, a role that critics say has become too sprawling.

item.title: Vance Issues Warning to Israeli Critics of U.S.-Iran Peace Agreement
item.description: Vice President JD Vance issued a warning to members of Israel’s government who were critical of the U.S.-Iran peace agreement on Thursday, cautioning them not to alienate their most important ally.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Jacquelyn Martin/Associated Press

item.title: Central Park Rides Halted After Fatal Fall From Horse-Drawn Carriage
item.description: An 18-year-old was ejected after a carriage horse bolted on Wednesday as the driver took a photograph. It was unclear how long the voluntary shutdown might last.
item.dc:creator: Ed Shanahan

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Police investigated the scene after a horse-drawn carriage overturned in Central Park on Wednesday.

item.title: James Talarico Attacks Ken Paxton Over Child Abuse Case
item.description: The case, which resulted in a short jail sentence despite years of alleged abuse, has drawn outcry in Texas. So have the attempts to politicize it.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy

media:content.media:credit: Callaghan O'Hare for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: James Talarico, the Democratic nominee for Senate in Texas, held a news conference to call on Ken Paxton to release files related to Adam Hoffman in Waco, Texas, on Thursday.

item.title: Is This Spielberg’s Greatest Era?
item.description: Wesley Morris invites his friend, the film curator Eric Hynes, to discuss “Disclosure Day” and the evolution of Steven Spielberg’s films.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Fralin, Alfredo Chiarappa, Jack Belisle, Daniele Sarti, Austin Mitchell and Felice Leon
item.category: Close Encounters of the Third Kind (Movie)

item.title: Vance’s Defense of Iran Deal Rests on Vague and Misleading Claims
item.description: The vice president said the United States had leverage to dictate the outcome of the next round of negotiations. But he claimed incorrectly that Iran got no new benefit from the lifting of oil sanctions.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Rebecca F. Elliott and Erica L. Green
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Vice President JD Vance during a briefing at the White House on Thursday.

item.title: During Trump’s Immigration Crackdown, Minnesota Protester Remained Shackled in Hospital for Days
item.description: The story of one progressive activist arrested in Minnesota in January shows what critics say is the aggressive nature of the Trump administration’s response to those who have protested its immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer

media:content.media:credit: Tim Gruber for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Paul Johnson, a longtime progressive activist, in the van where he alleges that federal agents assaulted him in January before leaving him shackled to a hospital bed for days before he was charged.

item.title: ‘Voicemails for Isabelle’ Review: An Unexpected Recipient
item.description: The movie, starring Zoey Deutch and Nick Robinson, begins as a tear-jerker and morphs into a poignant rom-com.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Kenny

item.title: Knicks Fans Celebrate With Ticker-Tape Parade
item.description: The ticker-tape parade for the New York Knicks championship took over downtown Manhattan and culminated with a ceremony at City Hall.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper, Jeremy Raff, Ang Li and David Cheung

media:content.media:credit: Lexi Parra/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Knicks celebrated their championship in front of hundreds of people at City Hall, after a parade watched by millions.

item.title: Inside Trump's Touring Exhibition of American Heroes
item.description: For a touring exhibition, Trump appointees chose 51 people who shaped our nation’s history. Among them: icons from the baby boomer era, Christians, wealthy industrialists and P.T. Barnum.
item.dc:creator: David A. Fahrenthold and Jeremy White

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Mangione Withdraws Emotional Disturbance Defense in New York Case
item.description: Luigi Mangione would have avoided the charge of second-degree murder if he were to convince a jury that strong emotions led to a “profound loss of self-control.”
item.dc:creator: Hurubie Meko

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis/Agence France-Presse, via Pool/Afp Via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Luigi Mangione in court on Wednesday, when a state judge announced his legal team’s intent to argue he was experiencing “extreme emotional disturbance.”

item.title: ‘Disclosure Day’ and How We Respond to the Unknown
item.description: Wesley Morris invites his friend, the film curator Eric Hynes, to consider the evolution of aliens in Steven Spielberg’s films.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Rocklin, Alfredo Chiarappa, Jack Belisle, Daniele Sarti, Felice Leon and Austin Mitchell
